posts.author: AMANDEEPSINHA
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: Although the demand for RIP bushings seems to be strong, other big players like Hitachi etc are also expanding capacity to meet the growing demand…Will it not be an issue for a small company like Yash?

posts.author: Debojit_Kangsa_Banik
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians

posts.author: ramansharma
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: It doesn’t seems to be concerning looking at the percentage w.r.t the total shareholding. They still own large chunk. Promoters have a track record of buying their shares from open market. Promoters have personal financial lives too like taxes, diversification, real estate, whatever. None of that means they’ve turned bearish on their own business.

posts.author: tbhavesh
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: Management recently sold 1.81L shares in open market - did not see any block deals here which means shares got offloaded to retail including HNIs - but this happened just days after management giving super good guidance and con call etc

posts.author: Debojit_Kangsa_Banik
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: Concall Q4FY26 Insights No impact of current war as no exports in those areas No impact on RM side as well, able to pass on the price Capable to grow at 40-42% for next 4-5 years concall3 830×327 43.2 KB This year Ebitda to remain around 24-25% coz of new factory Margin expansion likely from next year Revenue for totally indigenous made RIP bushings from our own factory might be towards the end of this year financial The revenue from this plant will still begin as we would be able to utilize the facility for assembly and testing from next month itself concall4 840×287 39.1 KB Present capacity…
